subject: Cloud Computing
title: Dropbox and the Storage Revolution
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Dropbox and the Storage Revolution.

hook.text: Drew Houston forgot his USB drive on a bus. He built Dropbox so nobody would need one again.

buildup.text: Dropbox stored your files in the cloud and synced them across every device automatically.

discovery.text: It launched in 2008 and reached 100 million users in five years with almost no advertising.

twist.text: Dropbox ran on AWS for years, then moved to its own servers to save $75 million over two years.

climax.text: A cloud storage company left the cloud because it got too expensive at massive scale.

punchline.text: The cloud isn't always cheapest. Dropbox did the math.

quiz.question: Why did Dropbox eventually move off AWS?
options: Running its own servers saved tens of millions of dollars at its scale
AWS refused to continue hosting Dropbox
Dropbox wanted to switch to Google Cloud instead
